source_jobs.title: HSE Engineer - North Macedonia
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in joining Alcazar Energy! Each role at our company offers the chance to make a tangible impact on the global shift towards sustainable energy. As you consider applying, reflect on how your unique skills, passion, and vision align with our mission to innovate and energize the world responsibly.

Responsibilities

Environmental and Social Management Systems

 - Monitor quality, health & safety, security and environmental performance of the Projects, conduct QHSSSE weekly inspections, identify non-compliances and opportunities for improvement, supervise the implementation of corrective actions and ensure all QHSSSE reporting requirements are met in full, and in a timely fashion (i.e. internal, external, regulatory) including but not limited:

 - Monthly QHSSSE Statistics preparation and/or review and coordination with the Contractors where applicable and provide the QHSSSE statistics approved to the E&S team for duly input in internal and external reports;

 - Conducting monthly HSE Inspections as well as quality audits; and

 - Monthly and quarterly reporting internally and externally.

 - Conduct investigations of any incidents or accidents when required

 - Review and ensure the adequate implementation of method statements and risk assessments by the Contractors

 - Promote, define and implement quality procedures in the project/s related to the construction and later on operation and maintenance activities of the project/s

 - Plan, manage, coordinate and evaluate the implementation of training sessions on QHSSSE systems and procedures to promote awareness and increase knowledge within the organisation as well as capacity building of Contractors and Subcontractors

 - Provide weekly updates (e.g. email, telephone calls) and monthly reports presenting the status of any open non-conformances, identifying any potential risks and areas for positive contribution

 - Operate in full compliance with internal policies and procedures as well as applicable regulations and laws, including but not limited to Code of Conduct, QHSE Policies, and Compliance Policies

Project Planning & Operations

 - Providing leadership and hands-on direction on QHSSSE aspects and promote a strong QHSSSE culture across the Project team from the Company to Contractors and Subcontractors.

 - Coordination of external visits including but not limited Lenders’ monitoring visits, Lenders’ E&S and Technical advisors audits, inspections by governmental authorities and social and cultural activities (such as educational visits)
   
   Social Development & Investment

 - Provide on-the-ground support to the D&O team on both QHSSSE and social aspects of the Projects as well as the implementation of the AEP’s ‘Social Development & Investment Program’ in the local communities to our projects

 - Build and maintain a positive and active relationship with Contractors and Subcontractors, communicating our strong commitment to occupational health & safety, environmental protection, respect for labour & human rights and strong governance, and fostering an environment that supports transparency and no fear of retaliation

 - Promote awareness of environmental, health & safety and social responsibility within the organisation by involving employees, ensuring both management and staff are informed of the status of non-conformances, opportunities for improvement and raising awareness on GIIP

Preferred Qualifications:

 - Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering (Electrical, Mechanical, Safety, Environmental or related field)

 - NEBOSH and/or IOSH Certifications

 - Quality Management Certificate/Training

Preferred Experience

 - Minimum of 7 years’ related experience in an HSE responsibility post

 - Previous experience in wind farms and/or solar photovoltaic plants

 - Preferably previous experience in a company with a strong QHSE culture

 - Preferably previous experience in internationally financed projects (that required meeting IFC Performance Stan

source_jobs.title: Environmental & Social Specialist – Egypt
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in joining Alcazar Energy! Each role at our company offers the chance to make a tangible impact on the global shift towards sustainable energy. As you consider applying, reflect on how your unique skills, passion, and vision align with our mission to innovate and energize the world responsibly.

Key Accountabilities

 - Building and maintaining a positive and active collaborative relationship with the landowners, local communities (and wider stakeholders), communicating our strong commitment to socio-economic development, environmental protection, respect for labour & human rights and strong governance, and fostering an environment that supports community involvement and ownership

 - Representative of Alcazar Energy for all E&S related matters

 - Being Alcazar Energy’s main local interface with E&S consultants, advisors, governmental authorities and other relevant stakeholders during the process of national environmental permitting and international Environmental & Social Impact Assessment (ESIA) and later on through the project life.

 - Coordinating the site-based tasks of E&S consultants during ESIA development, including, but not limited to, joining them on site visits, facilitating access to different stakeholders, such community members, NGOs, and local government representatives, and attending stakeholder meetings as the Company representative

 - Monitoring the progress of the ESIA process and review deliverables, to ensure Project requirements are fulfilled on time, within budget, with the right quality and in line with our E&S Compliance Framework

 - Planning and managing stakeholder engagement activities, disclosure of ESIA (and associated plans) and other E&S relevant information to local communities, and implementation of community related mitigation measures recommended in the ESIA

 - Participating in the development, regular review and implementation of the Stakeholder Engagement Plan (SEP)

 - Supporting in the coordination and taking part in Lenders’ Environmental and Social Due Diligence and periodical monitoring visits by the Lenders’/Lenders’ Environmental & Social Advisors and Technical Advisors

 - Being Alcazar Energy’s first point of contact for grievance management, in according with the Project’s Grievance Mechanism and being responsible for filtering grievances and ensuring they are addressed in a timely manner.

 - Monitoring and reporting on the E&S performance of consultants, service providers and engineering companies during the development phase of the Project

 - Monitoring and reporting on the E&S performance of the EPC and O&M Contractor (and its subcontractors) conducting E&S inspections, investigation for incidents and grievances, and following up on remedial and preventive actions

 - Responsible for overseeing the development of the Project-specific QHSEMS by the Contractor/s prior to the construction and operational phases

 - Ensuring the adequate implementation of the Project-specific QHSEMS by the Contractor/s (and their subcontractors) during construction and operation, in particular with relation to environmental protection measures, including pollution prevention waste management, third-party monitoring (if needed), among other aspects, as well as social aspects, including working conditions, employment terms, training , labour code of conduct and implementation of the worker grievance mechanism by the contractor.

 - Providing on-the-ground support to the Technical team as well as the Country/Business Development Manager and interface with the E&S and Technical corporate team and directing local resources as needed (such as the HSE Engineer) – Work closely with all parties to identify and address E&S risks, and material & emerging issues

 - Maintaining up to date knowledge of international, national and local environmental and social standards and legislation and the rapidly evolving ESG frameworks and requirements

 - Facilitating training session

source_jobs.title: Biodiversity Lead Expert
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in joining Alcazar Energy! Each role at our company offers the chance to make a tangible impact on the global shift towards sustainable energy. As you consider applying, reflect on how your unique skills, passion, and vision align with our mission to innovate and energize the world responsibly.

Biodiversity & Ecology Studies, Critical Habitat and Species Assessments

 - Conduct environmental screening assessments of potential investment opportunities at origination stage

 - Lead biodiversity sections of the E&S due diligence assessments, coordinating internal and third-party assessments by E&S consultants at early development stage

 - Support in the interface with Lenders E&S teams and Lenders E&S advisor

 - Lead and coordinate biodiversity, avifauna, and bat baseline studies for projects in the portfolio

 - Manage and review vantage point, acoustic, and seasonal survey programs

 - Support collision risk assessments (CRA) and migratory corridor assessments

 - Support Critical Habitat Assessments and protected area screening in line with IFC PS6, EBRD ES6 and EIB Standards

 - Review biodiversity and ornithology inputs to ESIA and related technical studies

Biodiversity Risk Management Across Project Planning & Operations

 - Provide early environmental support with planning and relevant consent applications

 - Integrate biodiversity, bird, and bat considerations into project siting, layout, and design

 - Apply the mitigation hierarchy (avoid, minimize, restore, offset) to project impacts

 - Develop and oversee species protection and mitigation measures, including turbine micro-siting, seasonal restrictions, and curtailment strategies

 - Oversee and monitor environmental and biodiversity performance of Turbine Supplier, Balance of Plant (BoP) and/or EPC and O&M contractor through site inspections, progress meetings, review of documentation, and performance indicators for Projects under construction and operation

 - Work closely with internal (E&S, technical, investments, contracts, legal) teams & external stakeholders to identify and address biodiversity risks

 - Support biodiversity risk management during construction and operation phases

 - Communicate project requirements, environmental & biodiversity procedures, and standards to relevant parties and oversee their implementation

 - Provide technical guidance to contractors and O&M teams on biodiversity requirements

Biodiversity Management Systems, Compliance, Monitoring & Reporting

 - Support in the development and implementation of the corporate ESMS ensuring adequate cascading across all AE portfolio projects at all stages

 - Oversee and support in the development of Project-specific ESMS to ensure alignment with ESIA, ESAP, Employer requirements, Investors and Lenders’ requirements

 - Develop and oversee implementation of Biodiversity Management Plans (BMPs) and Biodiversity Action Plans (BAPs) across all assets as applicable

 - Support lender and investor biodiversity due diligence, monitoring, and audits

 - Prepare and implement Avifauna and Bat Management Plans, including post-construction monitoring, turbine shutdown programs, fatality searches, and adaptive management

 - Support habitat restoration, enhancement, and biodiversity offset measures where required

 - Monitor effectiveness of mitigation and offset measures and support adaptive management

 - Coordinate with specialist consultants, regulators, and conservation stakeholders

 - Maintain up to date knowledge of international, regional, and local environmental and biodiversity standards of best practice, industry trends, and regulatory requirements

Biodiversity Governance, Stakeholder Engagement & Nature-Positive Initiatives

 - Integrate biodiversity requirements into corporate and project-level ESMS

 - Prepare biodiversity inputs to lender, regulatory, and ESG reports

 - Track biodiversity KPIs and monitor data across the portfolio

 - Contribute to natur

source_jobs.title: Construction Manager - Egypt
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in joining Alcazar Energy! Each role at our company offers the chance to make a tangible impact on the global shift towards sustainable energy. As you consider applying, reflect on how your unique skills, passion, and vision align with our mission to innovate and energize the world responsibly.

Reporting to: Project Execution Manager

Direct Reports: 2 (Site Engineers) / Site Consultants

 - Ensure that project site performance meets the required specifications, stays on schedule, adheres to contracts pricing and requirements, and follows company financial guidelines

 - Ensure compliance of project construction activities with Health, Safety & Environmental Regulations

 - Analyse, manage and mitigate project delivery risks

 - Oversee the performance on site, management of contractors involved and coordination with the rest of project team

 - Review the report on progress prepared by the Site Engineers and Owner’s Engineer

 - Review and validate the Contractor’s summary progress reports on construction and installation performance.

 - Coordinate and support to resolve any issues or problems related to logistics, heavy-lift operations, etc. that may have potential detrimental impacts of project progress as directed by the Project Execution Manager

 - Attend key progress, coordination and interface meetings with the contractors and owner’s engineer, report to Project Manager and Senior Management Team

 - Responsible for overseeing the contractors’ compliance with construction and erection activities specifications and standards, including civil works, electrical works and the installation of wind turbines

 - Responsible for overseeing the contractors’ compliance with construction specifications, technical standards, local regulations and Quality Management System

 - Support the Site Engineers as needed to review and prepare technical questions on the tender offer (support from the Engineering Team)

 - Ensure availability of programme indicating key environmental, planning, procurement, design, construction, commissioning and completion milestones

 - Oversee handover to O&M phase, ensuring all punch list items are completed and full site compliance is achieved

 - Manage resolution of any site technical matters on the projects that are escalated up from the Site Engineers

 - Ensure timely preparation, collection and handover of all as-built documentation, O&M manuals, commissioning certificates and record drawings

 - Support the Project Manager in managing contractual claims, variations and change orders

 - Contribute to the resolution of environmental, permitting and social issues at site level, coordinating when required with landowners, local communities, and authorities.

Qualification:

 - Degree in relevant engineering discipline; preferably Civil or Electrical
   
   PMP Certification or Project Management qualification is desirable

Experience:

 - 15 years of experience in managing site works for renewables or construction projects such as roads, overhead lines, substations and infrastructure; 5 of which at Construction Management level

 - Experience with managing international wind projects. Experience in Egypt is desirable

Job Specific Skills:

 - Ability to work with a high degree of autonomy and able to work with a team of project manager, support engineer and several contractors

 - Ability to plan and see the ‘big picture’

 - Strong communication abilities and fluency in English are required.

 - Excellent people management skills

 - Competent in conflict and crisis management –collaborative approach to solving issues with the community, contractors, colleagues

 - Understanding of quality assurance processes

 - Health, safety and environmental legislation applicable to construction projects

 - PC knowledge of Microsoft applications

 - Knowledge of specification, design, maintenance, construction and commissioning of construction works

 - Awareness of regulatory is

source_jobs.title: Project Engineer - Madrid
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in joining Alcazar Energy! Each role at our company offers the chance to make a tangible impact on the global shift towards sustainable energy. As you consider applying, reflect on how your unique skills, passion, and vision align with our mission to innovate and energize the world responsibly.

Key Responsibilities

 1.  Conduct wind resource assessments by analyzing meteorological data, performing energy yield calculations, and using industry-standard software (e.g., WAsP, WindPRO, OpenWind, or CFD models) to optimize wind farm layouts.

 2.  Support site selection and feasibility studies through wind measurement campaigns, GIS analysis, and data validation to identify high-potential locations for wind energy projects.

 3.  Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to provide technical insights on wind resource potential, uncertainty analysis, and energy production estimates, contributing to project development, financing, and operational decision-making.

 4.  Provide support on technical and commercial analysis for the Investment Team regarding renewable energy technologies, including onshore wind, solar PV and battery storage.

 5.  Support in the preparataion of reports and/or presentations for the Investment Team, or Investment Committee, summarising the analysis on potential technologies benefits, feasibility etc.

 6.  Support the Technical and Investment Team during the selection of the OEMs and Contractors for wind and solar projects, performing technical/commercial analysis etc.

 7.  Participate in technical Due Diligence for new Projects. This will include taking ownership of parts of the DD, and also supporting other members of the team and consultants that performing other parts of the DD.

 8.  Support in the preparation of tender request documents for projects including both commercial and technical requirements; inclusive of draft, release, clarification, evaluation and negotiation processes.

 9.  Participate in the dicussions with OEMs and Contractors to clarify contractor offers and meet the required Project requirements.

 10. Provide assessment of the technical components of potential investment opportunities

 11. To provide inputs to the investment Team (CAPEX, OPEX, energy yield, uncertainties, availability, construction period etc.) in order to run different scenarios on the financial models.

 12. To effectively manage engineering related service providers / consultants at the development stage of the project (preliminary designs, surveys etc.).

 13. Perform site visits to potential investment opportunities’ projects.

 14. Support the Investment Team in the search of new potential sites for future developments.

 15. Support the overall technical development of projects from early-stage screening to ready-to-build

 16. Contribute to defining project technical strategies and key design assumptions

Preferred Qualifications:

 - BSc. Degree in Engineering (Mechanical or Electrical)

 - Post Graduate degree in Renewable Energy is desirable

Preferred Experience:

 - Min. 5 year’s project engineering experience in Wind/Solar projects

 - Energy Yield Assessments

 - Fluency in both written and spoken in English at business level

Job Specific Skills:

 - Conversant with softwares such as Microsoft Project, PVSyst, WinPro, Autocad, etc.

We appreciate your time in considering a career with Alcazar Energy. By joining our team, you’re choosing to stand at the forefront of the renewable energy industry, contributing to projects that prioritize integrity, quality, and sustainability. As you share our commitment to making a difference, we look forward to potentially welcoming you aboard.

source_jobs.title: Senior Project Engineer - Madrid
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in joining Alcazar Energy! Each role at our company offers the chance to make a tangible impact on the global shift towards sustainable energy. As you consider applying, reflect on how your unique skills, passion, and vision align with our mission to innovate and energize the world responsibly.

Key Responsibilities



 - Provide all the required technical and commercial analysis to the Delivery and O&M and Investment Teams regarding renewable energy technologies, including mainly onshore wind, solar PV and battery storage. Coordinate the preparataion of reports and/or presentations for the Investment Team, or Investment Committee, summarising the analysis on potential technologies benefits, feasibility etc.

 - Lead the selection of the OEMs for wind and solar Projects, performing technical/commercial analysis etc.

 - Lead and perform technical Due Dilligence for new Projects. This will include takign ownership of parts of the DD, and to coordinate the support of project engineers, environemntal specialist, wind resource analyst and consultants that will perform the other parts of the DD.

 - To prepare RfP packages for surveys, engineering works, consultants, construction works, supplies etc. for projects including both commercial and technical requirements; inclusive of draft, release, clarification, evaluation and negotiation processes.

 - To prepare and negotiate Heads of Terms with EPC contactors.

 - Lead the dicussions with OEMs and contractors to optimise contractor offers and Project returns.

 - Provide support to the Investment Team for discussions with local developers and assessment of the technical components of potential investment opportunities.

 - To provide all required inputs to the investment Team (CAPEX, OPEX, energy yield, uncertainties, availability, construction period etc.) in order to run different scenarios on the financial models.

 - To effectively manage engineering related service providers / consultants at the development stage of the project (preliminary designs, surveys etc.).

 - Perform site visits for potential investment project opportunities.

 - Support the Investment Team in the search of new potential sites for future developments.

 - Coordinate the development activities of Projects (including designs, EPC selection, O&M contracts etc.) until the project teams are in place.

 - Conduct wind resource assessments by analyzing meteorological data, performing energy yield calculations, and using industry-standard software (e.g., WAsP, WindPRO, OpenWind, or CFD models) to optimize wind farm layouts.

 - Support site selection and feasibility studies through wind measurement campaigns, GIS analysis, and data validation to identify high-potential locations for wind energy projects.

 - Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to provide technical insights on wind resource potential, uncertainty analysis, and energy production estimates, contributing to project development, financing, and operational decision-making.

 - Implementation of turbine manufacturers contracts.



Qualifications, Experience & Job Specific skills



Preferred Qualifications:

 - BSc. Degree in Engineering (Civil, Mechanical or Electrical)

 - Post Graduate degree in Renewable Energy is desirable



Preferred Experience:

 - Experience in wind farms above 50 MW development is required

 - Experience in Solar PV Projects is desirable.

 - Experience working in engineering company with experience in windfarms design or WTG manufacturer is desirable.

 - Min. 10 year’s project engineering experience in Wind/Solar projects

 - Fluency in both written and spoken in English at a business level
   
   

Job Specific Skills:

 - Conversant with softwares such as Microsoft Project, PVSyst, WinPro etc.

 - Understand contracts Terms and Conditions (FIDIC etc.)

source_jobs.title: Senior Environmental and Social Specialist - Madrid or Dubai
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in joining Alcazar Energy! Each role at our company offers the chance to make a tangible impact on the global shift towards sustainable energy. As you consider applying, reflect on how your unique skills, passion, and vision align with our mission to innovate and energize the world responsibly.


RESPONSIBILITIES: ENVIRONMENTAL AND SOCIAL MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS

 - Support in the development and implementation of the corporate ESMS, ensuring adequate cascading across all AE portfolio of projects at all stages

 - Oversee and support in the development of project-specific ESMSs to ensure alignment with ESIA, ESAP, employer requirements, investors, and lenders’ requirements

 - Maintain up-to-date knowledge of international, regional, and local environmental and social standards and legislation, as well as rapidly evolving ESG frameworks and requirements

 - Coordinate/interface with internal resources or external consultants accordingly

 - Support in the required tasks to obtain and maintain ISO certifications



 - Environmental and Social Studies and Assessments
   
   - Conduct E&S screening assessments of potential investment opportunities at origination stage
   
   - Conduct E&S due diligence assessments, both internally and coordinating third party assessments by E&S consultants of potential investment opportunities
   
   - Support in the preparation of tenders, evaluation of proposals and selection of specialised consultants to perform ESIA studies and other related studies for projects
   
   - Coordinate the tasks of the E&S consultants, monitor the progress of the ESIA process and review deliverables, to ensure Project requirements are fulfilled on time, budget and with the right quality and up to the required standards (Corporate requirements and Lender standards)
   
   - Coordinate with the technical, project finance and investment team as well as in-country teams for the planning, execution and timely consideration of requirements
   
   - Support in the interface with Lenders E&S teams and Lenders E&S advisors
   
   - Coordinate required E&S activities at country level, directing local E&S representatives across all relevant E&S aspects including the process of land acquisition where applicable
   
   - Preparation of E&S components of tender submissions, evaluation and negotiation of E&S / QHSE contractual obligations with EPC / Suppliers / BOP and O&M contractors



Project Planning, Execution & Operations

 - Provide early environmental and social support with planning and relevant consent applications

 - Guide and manage the development of the Project-specific ESMMPs by the EPC / Suppliers / BOP and O&M contractors in line with applicable ESG compliance framework

 - Oversee and monitor E&S performance of EPC / Suppliers / BOP and O&M contractors through site inspections, progress meetings, review of documentation and performance indicators for Projects under construction and operation

 - Oversee the process followed by the Contractor to identify, correct and prevent incidents, accidents, non-conformances and grievances

 - Develop monthly, quarterly and annual E&S internal and external monitoring reports

 - Maintain strong liaison with the Project team at Corporate level, Country level and Project level

 - Work closely with internal (QHSE, technical, investments, contracts, legal) & external stakeholders to identify and address E&S risks, and material & emerging issues

 - Coordinate required E&S activities at country level, directing local E&S representatives

 - Communicate project requirements, environmental & social procedures and standards to relevant parties and oversee their implementation

 - Balance works to be completed internally or sub-contracted



Social Development & Investment

 - Support the development of the ‘social investment’ strategy in line with best international practice and specific operating guidelines (e.g. in human rights, community development fra

source_jobs.title: Lead, Marketing & Public Relations
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in joining Alcazar Energy! Each role at our company offers the chance to make a tangible impact on the global shift towards sustainable energy. As you consider applying, reflect on how your unique skills, passion, and vision align with our mission to innovate and energize the world responsibly.

Responsibilities:

• Cross-Platform Communication: Develop and implement communication strategies that highlight the benefits of emerging markets renewable energy and Alcazar’s energy platform, supported by in-depth market research, trend analysis, and content creation. Deliver commentaries and short-form content across multiple channels, including conferences, presentations, and digital platforms.

• Content Creation: Write and manage the production of materials from concept to completion across diverse formats—white papers, articles, videos, podcasts, and social media. Ensuring consistency in messaging, voice, and editorial quality.

• Public Relations and Media Strategy: Develop and execute a proactive PR strategy that enhances Alcazar's reputation in the private equity and renewable energy sectors. Cultivate relationships with key media outlets, industry influencers, and thought leaders to secure coverage and position Alcazar as a leader in sustainable infrastructure investment. Manage crisis communication and respond to media inquiries as needed.

• Rebranding and Marketing Initiatives: Lead Alcazar Energy’s rebranding efforts, including the development of a refreshed brand identity that aligns with our strategic vision. Oversee marketing initiatives designed to elevate Alcazar's reputation within the renewable energy space, targeting key investor audiences and developers with bespoke content and branding efforts.

• Private Equity/renewable energy Marketing: Create compelling marketing collateral that articulates Alcazar’s unique selling points in sustainable infrastructure to the private equity

and investment community. This includes materials for investor presentations, offering documents, and updates on fund performance, as well as ESG and sustainability initiatives.

• Industry Analysis: Monitor and synthesise key trends in renewable energy, sustainable infrastructure, and private equity, particularly in emerging markets, for use in Alcazar’s communication channels and investor outreach.

• Stakeholder Engagement: Work closely with Senior Leadership, investment professionals, and internal teams to align marketing efforts with broader business strategies. Collaborate with external agencies, designers, and media outlets where necessary.

• Metrics and Reporting: Produce regular metrics reports that track the effectiveness of marketing campaigns and communication efforts, with a focus on investor engagement, and brand visibility.

Relationship Management:

• Build and maintain strong relationships with both internal stakeholders (Senior Leadership, investment professionals) and external stakeholders (investors, developers, media, and agencies).



Qualifications and Skills:

• 10+ years of PR/marketing and communications experience within asset management or private equity, with a focus on renewable energy or infrastructure, with proven content creation, project management, and strategic positioning capabilities.

• Marketing degree preferred; CFA or equivalent certifications advantageous.

• Expertise in infrastructure or renewable energy investment management products and services, ideally across multiple distribution channels. A strong interest or experience in ESG and emerging markets is highly desirable.

• Strong multitasking abilities and comfort working in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment. Must be adept at managing ambiguity and complexity.

• Excellent interpersonal skills, with an ability to cultivate lasting relationships with senior executives and investors.

• Demonstrated organisational, prioritisation, and time-management skills, with a focus on detail-oriented execution.

• A

source_jobs.title: Accountant - N Macedonia
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in joining Alcazar Energy! Each role at our company offers the chance to make a tangible impact on the global shift towards sustainable energy. As you consider applying, reflect on how your unique skills, passion, and vision align with our mission to innovate and energize the world responsibly.

Responsibilities:

· Prepares asset, liability, and capital account entries by compiling and analysing account information.

· Documents financial transactions by entering accounting entries in the ERP/Accounting system.

· Recommends financial actions by analysing accounting options.

· Summarizes current financial status by collecting information, preparing balance sheet, profit and loss statement, and other reports.

· Substantiates financial transactions by auditable documents.

· Maintains accounting controls by adhering to policies and procedures.

· Reconciles financial discrepancies by collecting and analyzing information.

· Maintains financial security by following internal controls.

· Prepares payments by verifying documentation and requesting disbursements.

· Prepares ad hoc financial reports by collecting, analysing, and summarizing account information and trends.

· Maintains confidentiality and protects operations by keeping financial information confidential

· Coordinate with auditor to finalise external financial audits.

· Accomplishes the result by performing the duty.

· Contributes to team effort by accomplishing related results as needed.

· Support line Manager with special projects and workflow process improvements.





Qualifications:

 - Bachelor’s degree in accounting



EXPERIENCE:

 - 3 years audit and 3+ year’s prior experience in Corporate Accounting/general ledger/project accounting/project costing.

 - Experience working in a large organization/MNC is preferred.

 - Experience working on ERP desirable

 - Experience in producing management/SH reports would be an advantage



JOB SPECIFIC SKILLS:

 - Must be PC proficient and able to thrive in a fast -pace setting

 - Advanced MS Excel skills

 - Financial sensitivity & acumen

 - Strong ability to sense check numbers confirm accuracy of financial statements and reconcile outstanding balances of selected accounts

 - Above-average system literate and capable of gradually taking ownership beyond the direct accounting tasks assigned

 - Ability to effectively manage multiple priorities, be deadline-oriented and possess multi-tasking skills

 - Attentive to detail

 - Strong verbal and written communication, interpersonal and team skills required

 - Must be able to maintain confidentiality

 - High energy level and presence

 - Demonstrated ability to initiate and structure

 - Exposure to processing high volume of invoices/journals/transactions (50/60+)

 - Exposure to audit environment and understanding of documentation necessary for compliance

 - Strong understanding of management accounting and end to end accounting process (AP, AR, bank recs, accruals, prepayments, depreciation/FA



We appreciate your time in considering a career with Alcazar Energy. By joining our team, you’re choosing to stand at the forefront of the renewable energy industry, contributing to projects that prioritize integrity, quality, and sustainability. As you share our commitment to making a difference, we look forward to potentially welcoming you aboard.

notes: Energy company with a mix of technical and non-technical roles, mostly focused on operations and project management.
